skill.name: building-nextjs-apps
skill.description: Build Next.js 16 applications with correct routing, caching, proxy, datetime, and frontend design patterns.
skill.summary: Guides Next.js 16 app builds, migrations, and UI implementation patterns.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, and blocker patterns, including a critical combined heuristic. Review found no confirmed malicious intent; most hits are Markdown examples or prose. The skill still needs warnings for unpinned package execution guidance and an insecure auth-token response-header example.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned Package Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends npm and npx commands that execute packages from remote registries. This is normal for Next.js work, but @latest increases supply-chain risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in setup guidance and can execute remote packages. The intent appears legitimate, but version pinning is absent.

medium_findings.title: Auth Token Added to Response Header
medium_findings.description: One proxy example sets x-user-token from a session cookie on the response. This can expose a credential to the browser or intermediaries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example reads a session cookie and writes the token into a response header. This is a clear insecure pattern, even if shown as guidance.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command hits are Markdown fences or inline code spans. No Ruby backtick execution source was found in those cited examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown text and fenced examples, not executable Ruby files. The scanner pattern is a strong false positive.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Network and Environment Examples
low_findings.description: Fetch and process.env examples use example domains, localhost, or developer-configured backend URLs. No hardcoded exfiltration endpoint was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples show normal application fetch patterns and environment configuration. They do not send secrets to an attacker-controlled URL.

low_findings.title: Blocker Labels Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: Weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts map to headings or design prose. No cryptographic API or system reconnaissance instruction was found there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions and headings. The static labels do not match the semantic content of the lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned Package Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends npm and npx commands that execute packages from remote registries. This is normal for Next.js work, but @latest increases supply-chain risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in setup guidance and can execute remote packages. The intent appears legitimate, but version pinning is absent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Auth Token Added to Response Header
dangerous_patterns.description: One proxy example sets x-user-token from a session cookie on the response. This can expose a credential to the browser or intermediaries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example reads a session cookie and writes the token into a response header. This is a clear insecure pattern, even if shown as guidance.

content.value_statement: Next.js 16 changed routing, caching, proxy, and async request APIs. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused patterns for correct app builds.
actual_capabilities: Explains async params and searchParams patterns for pages, layouts, and route handlers.
Shows proxy.ts usage that replaces middleware.ts in Next.js 16.
Documents Turbopack and cacheComponents migration changes.
Provides datetime-local to UTC conversion patterns for full-stack apps.
Includes frontend aesthetic guidance for more distinctive interfaces.
Lists Next.js DevTools MCP setup and common diagnostic uses.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include a runnable Next.js project.
Some package setup commands use @latest and should be pinned before production use.
Examples use placeholder APIs and require project-specific validation.
It focuses on Next.js and React, not backend-only applications.

use_cases.title: Create a New App Router Project
use_cases.description: Use the skill to scaffold pages, layouts, routes, server actions, and API handlers with Next.js 16 patterns.

use_cases.title: Upgrade a Next.js 15 Codebase
use_cases.description: Use the migration checklist to update async params, proxy.ts, Turbopack config, cacheComponents, and image usage.

use_cases.title: Improve Product Interface Quality
use_cases.description: Use the design guidance to choose typography, color, motion, and layout choices with stronger visual direction.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Next.js 16 Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Next.js 16 page with async params, a layout, and a simple data loading plan.

prompt_templates.title: Add Auth Proxy Routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a secure proxy.ts and API route pattern for authenticated backend requests in Next.js 16.

prompt_templates.title: Upgrade from Next.js 15
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Next.js 15 app and list every change needed for Next.js 16 compatibility.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Design and implement a distinctive Next.js 16 dashboard using the frontend aesthetic guidelines.

output_examples.input: Create a dynamic product page for Next.js 16.
output_examples.output: A page plan with async params, metadata handling, server data loading, and route error states.

output_examples.input: Upgrade my app from Next.js 15 to 16.
Make this dashboard feel less generic.
output_examples.output: A focused design direction with typography, color, motion, and layout recommendations for the product context.

best_practices: Pin package versions before running generated setup commands.
Validate generated API examples against your authentication and data model.
Use the migration checklist before changing framework configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder backend URLs into production settings.
Do not expose session tokens in response headers or client-readable data.
Do not use Next.js 15 params patterns in Next.js 16 routes.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Next.js 16 apps with App Router, proxy.ts, caching, routing, and frontend design guidance.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete project?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and instructions that an AI coding tool can apply inside your project.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with warnings. Review package commands and authentication examples before applying them.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: It includes unpinned package execution guidance and one insecure token header example.

faq.question: Can Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What projects should avoid it?
faq.answer: Avoid it for non-React projects, backend-only services, or apps not using Next.js App Router.
